entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench, the crop species studied for its developmental response to photoperiod and temperature.

entities.name: PHOTOPERIOD
entities.description: The duration of light exposure in a 24-hour period that influences plant development.

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: An environmental factor that influences plant development throughout its entire life cycle.

entities.name: PANICLE INITIATION
entities.description: The morphological change where the shoot apex becomes a shiny globular structure, marking the end of the vegetative stage.

entities.name: PHOTOPERIOD SENSITIVITY
entities.description: The capacity of sorghum cultivars to respond to different day lengths to regulate the timing of panicle initiation.

entities.name: VEGETATIVE STAGE
entities.description: The growth phase of the plant extending from seedling emergence to panicle initiation.

entities.name: SEEDLING EMERGENCE
entities.description: The point at which the plant first appears above the soil surface.

entities.name: SHORT DAYS
entities.description: A specific photoperiod condition defined as 8 hours of light per day.

entities.name: LONG DAYS
entities.description: A specific photoperiod condition defined as 17 hours of light per day.

entities.name: CROP SIMULATION MODELS
entities.description: Computational tools used to simulate and predict plant development based on environmental and genetic factors.

entities.name: PHOTOPERIOD SENSITIVITY GENES
entities.description: Genes that control the sensitivity of sorghum to photoperiod.
